title: 7 Things to Know About How Bangladeshi Freelancers Accept International Payments Before You Start
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Imagine you're a Bangladeshi freelancer creating digital products, but every time you try to collect payment from international clients, your bank freezes the transaction, and you're left without the money you desperately need. You've tried using PayPal, but it's blocked in Bangladesh, and Stripe is no better. You've heard about Web3 Wallet Connect, but it sounds complicated, and you're not sure where to start. You're not alone – many freelancers in restricted countries face the same struggles. It's time to break free from these limitations and learn how to accept international payments like a pro.
The Full List With Context

When it comes to accepting international payments, there are several key factors to consider. Here are the top seven things you need to know before you start: 1. **Know your payment options**: Research alternative payment processors that allow international transactions, such as NOWPayments. 2. **Choose a reliable payment gateway**: Select a gateway that supports multiple currencies, has low fees, and offers robust security features. 3. **Understand cross-border fees**: Be aware of the fees associated with international transactions and factor them into your pricing. 4. **Comply with anti-money laundering (AML) regulations**: Implement AML measures to prevent fraudulent transactions and maintain a clean reputation. 5. **Set up a secure payment system**: Use a payment system that encrypts sensitive information and offers two-factor authentication. 6. **Communicate with your clients**: Clearly explain your payment policies and procedures to your clients to avoid misunderstandings. 7. **Stay up-to-date with regulatory changes**: Monitor changes in payment regulations and adjust your payment setup accordingly. Each of these factors plays a crucial role in ensuring smooth international transactions and maintaining a positive reputation.
Why Each Item on This List Matters

Each item on this list is essential for a seamless international payment experience. For example, choosing a reliable payment gateway can save you from costly transaction failures and reputational damage. Understanding cross-border fees can help you set competitive prices and avoid losing clients. Complying with AML regulations demonstrates your commitment to ethics and security, which is essential for building trust with clients. Setting up a secure payment system protects sensitive information from cyber threats, while communicating with your clients ensures that they understand your payment policies. Lastly, staying up-to-date with regulatory changes helps you avoid unexpected disruptions to your business.
The Ones Most Creators Get Wrong

One of the most common mistakes Bangladeshi freelancers make is underestimating the importance of AML regulations. Ignoring these regulations can lead to serious consequences, including account closures and damage to your reputation. Another mistake is choosing a payment gateway that doesn't support multiple currencies or has high fees. This can lead to lost revenue and frustrated clients. Additionally, many creators fail to communicate their payment policies clearly, leading to misunderstandings and disputes with clients. Lastly, neglecting to stay up-to-date with regulatory changes can leave you vulnerable to unexpected disruptions.
The blockchain has no map of restricted countries — a wallet in Lagos processes identically to one in London.
How to Prioritise What to Implement First

If you're new to international payments, it's essential to prioritize your efforts. Start by researching alternative payment processors, such as NOWPayments, and choosing a reliable payment gateway. Once you've set up a secure payment system, focus on complying with AML regulations and communicating your payment policies clearly to clients. As you gain more experience, you can explore additional features, such as cross-border fee management and regulatory updates. By prioritizing these key factors, you'll be well on your way to accepting international payments like a pro.
Putting the List Into Action

Now that you know the top seven things to consider when accepting international payments, it's time to put them into action. Start by researching alternative payment processors and choosing a reliable payment gateway. Set up a secure payment system and comply with AML regulations. Communicate your payment policies clearly to clients and stay up-to-date with regulatory changes. By following these steps, you'll be able to accept international payments with confidence and grow your business. Don't forget to check out Web3 Wallet Connect, a powerful tool that can help you streamline your payment process and reduce costs.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most practical path for creators in restricted countries is a combination of NOWPayments for payment processing and USDT on Tron for the primary payment coin. USDT is a stable coin pegged to the US dollar, which means your revenue does not fluctuate with crypto market movements. Tron network fees are under one dollar, and settlement takes under 60 seconds. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.

Your Action Checklist

Here's a concrete action plan to get you started: 1. Research alternative payment processors, such as NOWPayments, and choose a reliable payment gateway. 2. Set up a secure payment system and comply with AML regulations. 3. Communicate your payment policies clearly to clients and stay up-to-date with regulatory changes. 4. By following these steps, you'll be well on your way to accepting international payments like a pro.
Geographic neutrality is not a feature of crypto payments — it is the fundamental property of how blockchains work.
